# dfweb-v4

> Portfolio website built with Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript, and Sanity CMS

## Overview

Modern, accessible portfolio website showcasing projects and CV. Features server-side rendering, Matrix-inspired design, comprehensive testing infrastructure, and multi-layered security hardening.

**Live URL**: https://www.dfweb.no

## Project Structure

```
src/
├── app/                    # Next.js App Router pages
│   ├── page.tsx           # Homepage (/)
│   ├── cv/                # CV page
│   ├── kontakt/           # Contact page
│   └── prosjekter/        # Projects page
├── components/            # React components (.component.tsx suffix)
│   ├── Animations/        # Matrix animations, loaders
│   ├── CV/               # CV-specific components
│   ├── ErrorBoundary/    # Error handling
│   ├── Index/            # Homepage components
│   ├── Kontakt/          # Contact form
│   ├── Layout/           # Header, Footer, Navigation
│   ├── Prosjekter/       # Project cards
│   └── UI/               # Reusable UI components
├── __tests__/            # Jest tests (mirrors src/ structure)
├── e2e/                  # End-to-end tests
│   ├── cypress/          # Cypress tests (non-standard location)
│   └── playwright/       # Playwright tests (non-standard location)
├── hooks/                # Custom React hooks
└── lib/                  # Utilities, Sanity client
    ├── csrf.ts
    ├── framer/           # Motion/Framer utilities
    └── sanity/           # Sanity CMS client and queries
studio/                    # Sanity Studio CMS
public/                    # Static assets
```

## Tech Stack

### Core
- **Framework**: Next.js 16 with App Router, Server Components, Turbopack dev server
- **Language**: TypeScript (strict mode, 100% typed)
- **UI Library**: React 19
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS 4, CSS Modules
- **CMS**: Sanity.io (headless)
- **Animations**: Motion (formerly Framer Motion)
- **Forms**: React Hook Form + Zod validation
- **Package Manager**: pnpm 11.15.1

### Testing & Quality
- **Unit Testing**: Jest 30, React Testing Library
- **E2E Testing**: Cypress 15, Playwright
- **Accessibility**: Pa11y, Cypress Axe
- **Performance**: Lighthouse CI
- **Code Quality**: ESLint, Prettier, Stylelint
- **Security**: Snyk, Trivy, Semgrep, CodeQL, Gitleaks

### DevOps
- **CI/CD**: GitHub Actions (SHA-pinned), CircleCI
- **Monitoring**: Vercel Speed Insights, Codecov
- **Documentation**: Ladle, Repomix, AGENTS.md

## Non-Obvious Patterns (CRITICAL)

### Component Naming Convention
- Components use `.component.tsx` suffix (NOT standard React)
- Example: `Button.component.tsx`, `Header.component.tsx`, `Section.component.tsx`

### AAA Pattern Enforcement (MANDATORY)
All tests MUST include these comments or builds fail:
```typescript
// Arrange
// Act  
// Assert
```
- Enforced by custom ESLint plugin at `src/utils/eslint/`
- Runtime validation in `jest.setup.ts` using `src/utils/test-utils.ts`
- Tests outside `src/e2e/` directory must follow this pattern
- Cypress and Playwright have different ESLint rule overrides

### Turbopack Dev Server
- Uses `--turbopack` flag (non-standard Next.js setup)
- Command: `pnpm dev`

### Custom CSP Headers
- Allows `https://presentasjon.dfweb.no` for iframe embedding
- Configured in `next.config.ts` with strict Content Security Policy

### Sanity Configuration
- Hardcoded fallback values in `src/lib/sanity/client.ts`:
  - Project ID: `41s7iutf`
  - Dataset: `production`
  - API Version: `2026-05-01`

### Test Coverage Exclusions
- `Matrix.component.tsx` intentionally excluded from coverage via package.json script
- Animations/Matrix types also excluded

### E2E Test Structure
- Cypress tests in `src/e2e/cypress/` (not standard root-level location)
- Playwright tests in `src/e2e/playwright/` (not standard root-level location)

### Custom Refresh Script
- `pnpm refresh` does full cleanup including store prune and lock file removal
- More aggressive than standard `pnpm install`

## Critical Commands

### Development
```bash
pnpm dev              # Start dev server (uses --turbopack)
pnpm build            # Production build
pnpm start            # Start production server
pnpm lint             # Run ESLint
pnpm lint:css         # Lint CSS files
pnpm lint:css:fix     # Fix CSS lint issues
pnpm format           # Format with Prettier
pnpm refresh          # Full cleanup and reinstall
```

### Testing
```bash
# Unit Tests
pnpm test                    # Run all tests with coverage
pnpm test:watch              # Jest watch mode
jest --testNamePattern="name" # Run single test (no built-in script)

# E2E Tests
pnpm cypress:open            # Open Cypress UI
pnpm cypress:headless        # Run Cypress headless
pnpm e2e                     # Run E2E tests (requires dev server)
pnpm playwright              # Run Playwright tests

# Coverage (with Matrix exclusion)
jest --coverage --collectCoverageFrom='src/components/**/*.{js,jsx,ts,tsx}' --collectCoverageFrom='!src/components/Animations/Matrix.component.{js,jsx,ts,tsx}'
```

### Performance & Monitoring
```bash
pnpm lhci             # Lighthouse CI (all checks)
pnpm lhci:perf        # Performance-only
pnpm lhci:desktop     # Desktop checks
pnpm doctor           # React health check
```

### CMS & Documentation
```bash
pnpm typegen          # Generate Sanity types
pnpm ladle            # Component documentation
```

## Environment Variables

### Required
```env
# Email Configuration (client-side, EmailJS)
NEXT_PUBLIC_EMAIL_API_KEY=user_xxx
NEXT_PUBLIC_EMAIL_TEMPLATE_KEY=template_xxx
NEXT_PUBLIC_EMAIL_SERVICE_KEY=service_xxx

# Security (server-side)
CSRF_SECRET=your-secure-csrf-secret-key

# Sanity (optional, has fallbacks)
NEXT_PUBLIC_SANITY_PROJECT_ID=41s7iutf
NEXT_PUBLIC_SANITY_DATASET=production
NEXT_PUBLIC_SANITY_API_VERSION=2026-05-01
```

## Key Features

### Performance
- 100/100 Lighthouse scores (all metrics)
- Server Components for optimal SSR
- Streaming with Suspense boundaries
- Automatic static optimization with revalidation
- LCP of 0.18s on `/prosjekter` via preloading, React.cache, and server-only patterns
- Skeleton loaders with shimmer effects

### Design & UX
- Matrix-inspired design with canvas rain effect
- Custom animated cursor with matrix trail effect
- Fully responsive (mobile, laptop, ultra-wide tested)
- Tailwind 4 with CSS custom-property design tokens in `globals.css`
- Polymorphic components
- Error boundaries with fallback UI

### Accessibility
- WCAG2AA compliance (Pa11y automated testing)
- Full keyboard navigation (WAI-ARIA APG patterns)
- Visible focus rings (`:focus-visible`)
- Skip links, ARIA labels
- Builds fail if accessibility errors found

### Security (Multi-Layered)
- Content Security Policy headers
- CSRF protection
- OWASP scanning (Semgrep with custom rules in `.semgrep/`)
- Secret detection (Gitleaks)
- Dependency scanning (Snyk, Trivy)
- Supply chain hardening (see below)

## Testing Gotchas

1. **AAA Pattern**: Enforced by BOTH ESLint rule AND runtime validation
2. **Matrix Exclusion**: Matrix.component.tsx excluded from coverage intentionally
3. **Test Location**: Test files must mirror source structure
4. **E2E Rules**: Different ESLint overrides for Cypress vs Playwright

## Code Quality Principles

Follows SOLID, DRY, KISS, YAGNI principles (see AGENTS.md for full details)

### Key Rules
- **Single Responsibility**: Separate UI from business logic
- **Open/Closed**: Extend via composition, not modification
- **TypeScript**: Comprehensive types for all props/functions
- **Accessibility-First**: Native semantics before ARIA
- **Performance-First**: Minimize re-renders, use Server Components
- **Test-First**: 100% coverage target with AAA pattern

## UI Development Rules (Condensed)

### Interactions
- ✅ Full keyboard support, visible focus rings
- ✅ Hit targets ≥24px (mobile ≥44px)
- ✅ No blocking paste, support password managers
- ✅ URL reflects state (deep-linkable)
- ✅ Enter submits text input; Cmd/Ctrl+Enter submits textarea

### Animation
- ✅ Honor `prefers-reduced-motion`
- ✅ Animate compositor props (`transform`, `opacity`)
- ✅ Interruptible, input-driven animations

### Accessibility
- ✅ Accessible color contrast (prefer APCA over WCAG 2)
- ✅ Icon-only buttons need `aria-label`
- ✅ Native semantics before ARIA
- ✅ Redundant status cues (not color-only)

### Performance
- ✅ Track re-renders (React DevTools/React Scan)
- ✅ Virtualize large lists
- ✅ Preload above-fold images, lazy-load rest
- ✅ Prevent CLS from images

## Security — Supply Chain Defense

**CRITICAL**: Multi-layered attack prevention. Preserve these measures when modifying configs or workflows.

### Package Installation Defenses
| Defense | Config | Notes |
|---------|--------|-------|
| `ignore-scripts=true` | `.npmrc`, `studio/.npmrc` | Blocks post-install scripts |
| `allowBuilds` whitelist | `pnpm-workspace.yaml` | Explicit opt-in for native packages |
| `minimum-release-age=2880` | `.npmrc` | Blocks packages <48h old (manual) |
| 7-day release age | `renovate.json` | Automated updates wait 7 days |
| `block-exotic-subdependencies=true` | `.npmrc` | Prevents non-registry sources |
| `--frozen-lockfile` | All CI workflows | Prevents lockfile modification |
| `engine-strict=true` | `.npmrc` | Enforces Node.js version |

### CI/CD Security
| Defense | Config | Notes |
|---------|--------|-------|
| SHA-pinned GitHub Actions | All workflows | Never use floating tags |
| No `pull_request_target` | All workflows | Cache poisoning prevention |
| Least-privilege permissions | All workflows | Explicit `permissions` blocks |
| No `restore-keys` fallbacks | Cache steps | Prevents poisoned cache |

### Security Scanning
- **Snyk**: Dependency vulnerabilities + monitoring
- **Trivy**: Filesystem scanning (CRITICAL/HIGH)
- **Semgrep**: SAST (OWASP, JS, TS, React + custom `.semgrep/` rules)
- **CodeQL**: GitHub native security-and-quality
- **Gitleaks**: Secret detection

### Agent Rules (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
- ❌ NEVER add `pull_request_target` to workflows
- ❌ NEVER use floating tags for GitHub Actions (always SHA-pin)
- ❌ NEVER use `npm install -g <package>` in CI without pinned version
- ❌ NEVER add `restore-keys` fallbacks to cache steps
- ❌ NEVER remove `ignore-scripts=true` from `.npmrc`
- ✅ ALWAYS use `--frozen-lockfile` for pnpm in CI
- ✅ ALWAYS add explicit `permissions` blocks to workflows
- ✅ When adding packages needing build scripts, add to `allowBuilds` in `pnpm-workspace.yaml`

Full hardening plan: `plans/supply-chain-hardening.md`

## AI-Friendly Documentation

### Primary Resources
- **AGENTS.md**: Main agent guidance (patterns, principles, gotchas)
- **DOCS/README.md**: Technical documentation and architecture
- **DOCS/repository_context.txt**: Auto-generated full codebase context
- **DOCS/TEST_STRATEGY.md**: ISTQB-aligned test strategy
- **README.md**: Project overview and features

### Mode-Specific Rules
- `.roo/rules-code/AGENTS.md`: Code mode guidance
- Custom ESLint plugin: `src/utils/eslint/`

### Automated Updates
- Repomix workflow generates `repository_context.txt` on every push to main
- AI-powered commit messages via Python script in `commit/`

## Important Notes

### Data Fetching
- Server Components for data fetching
- `sanityFetch<T>()` helper with 24h default revalidation
- React.cache for deduplication
- Preloading pattern in `/prosjekter`

### Contact Form
- Email.js integration
- CSRF protection via `lib/csrf.ts`
- React Hook Form + Zod validation
- Reusable GenericForm component pattern

### Error Handling
- react-error-boundary for component errors
- Custom ErrorBoundary with fallback UI
- Development mode error triggers
- Comprehensive error logging

### Sitemap
- Automatic generation (`src/app/sitemap.ts`)
- Google rich results for each page
- SEO optimized

## Performance Achievements

- **Lighthouse**: 100/100/100/100 (Performance, Accessibility, Best Practices, SEO)
- **LCP**: 0.18s on `/prosjekter` page
- **Test Coverage**: 100% (Jest + React Testing Library)
- **Accessibility**: WCAG2AA compliant
- **React Doctor Score**: >80 threshold enforced

## Quick Reference

### File Patterns
- Components: `*.component.tsx`
- Tests: `*.test.tsx` or `*.test.ts`
- Styles: CSS Modules or Tailwind classes
- Types: TypeScript interfaces/types (generated from Sanity)

### Key Directories
- `/src/app/*`: Pages and routes
- `/src/components/*`: UI components
- `/src/lib/*`: Core utilities
- `/src/hooks/*`: Custom React hooks
- `/src/e2e/*`: E2E test suites
- `/studio/*`: Sanity CMS studio
- `/.github/workflows/*`: CI/CD pipelines

### Configuration Files
- `next.config.ts`: Next.js config (CSP, images, headers)
- `jest.config.ts`: Jest test config
- `jest.setup.ts`: Test setup + AAA validation
- `cypress.config.ts`: Cypress E2E config
- `playwright.config.ts`: Playwright config
- `.npmrc`: pnpm + security config
- `pnpm-workspace.yaml`: Workspace + allowBuilds
- `renovate.json`: Automated dependency updates
- `tailwind.config.ts`: Tailwind CSS config
- `tsconfig.json`: TypeScript config

## Learn More

For comprehensive coding guidance, read these in order:
1. **AGENTS.md** - Start here for non-obvious patterns
2. **README.md** - Feature overview and badges
3. **DOCS/README.md** - Technical deep dive
4. **DOCS/TEST_STRATEGY.md** - Testing methodology
